texto: It's so frustrating that everybody rags on eighties music, when there's a lot of terrific stuff out there," he said. "There's no irony to it. It's not afraid to just be happy or enthusiastic or earnest. Or to have melody. Sure, you can blame it for being naive, but isn't that refreshing next to the whiny navel-gazing that came after it?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Amanda DeWees que provoca temas ligados a felicidade. Ao questionar “It's so frustrating that everybody rags on eighties music, when there's a lot of terrific stuff o…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre felicidade, earnest, eighties, com foco em eighties e enthusiastic.

frase_original: It's so frustrating that everybody rags on eighties music, when there's a lot of terrific stuff out there," he said. "There's no irony to it. It's not afraid to just be happy or enthusiastic or earnest. Or to have melody. Sure, you can blame it for being naive, but isn't that refreshing next to the whiny navel-gazing that came after it?

texto: He stood at the foot of the grave, gloved hands clasped behind him, his dark clothes and hair blending into one black silhouette, as if he were not a presence but an absence, a hole cut out of the landscape.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Amanda DeWees: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “He stood at the foot of the grave, gloved hands clasped behind him, his dark clothes and hair ble…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, gothic-romance, historical-romance pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e reflexao-noturna.

texto: The ability to lie persuasively is one of the greatest gifts a woman can possess in this life. Some critics, principally men, will argue that deception in women is inherently evil; but having spent the last fifteen years of my life in the theater, I can attest that lying not only is sometimes expedient but can save one's career.

Some critics, principally men, will argue that deception in women is inherently evil; but having spent the last fifteen years of my life in the theater, I can attest that lying not only is sometimes expedient but can save one's career.

texto: The wavelets flung themselves up as if trying to pat my feet and I darted back, laughing, and picked up my skirts to chase them back as they receded, in a game of tag more ancient than I then knew.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Amanda DeWees: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a amor.
